---
title: "Call for Speakers"
---

The **Transdisciplinary Artificial Intelligence Hub (TAIH)** is seeking dynamic and engaging speakers for the inaugural TAIH Symposium 2026.

We are looking for researchers, industry professionals, and thought leaders who are pushing the boundaries of AI, transparent methodologies, and real-world applications.

## Presentation formats

We are currently accepting proposals for the following formats:

* **Keynote presentations** (45 minutes + 15 minutes Q&A): Visionary talks on the future of AI and transdisciplinary research.
* **Technical talks** (20 minutes + 10 minutes Q&A): Deep dives into specific methodologies, case studies, or recent publications.
* **Lightning talks** (5-7 minutes): Quick, high-energy presentations highlighting early-stage research or novel ideas.
* **Panelists:** We are also seeking experts to participate in our afternoon panel discussion bridging industry and academia.

## Topics of interest

While we are open to a wide range of AI-related topics, we are particularly interested in talks addressing:

* Ethical AI and governance
* AI in healthcare / energy / sustainability
* Explainability and trustworthy models
* Cross-disciplinary applications of machine learning

## How to apply
To submit a speaking proposal, please prepare a brief abstract (max. 300 words) and a short speaker bio.

**[Link to Google Form / Submission Portal TBD]**

Alternatively, you can email your proposal directly to our organizing committee at [transdisciplinaryaihub@gmail.com](mailto:transdisciplinaryaihub@gmail.com).

## Important dates

* **Speaker proposal deadline:** [Date TBD]
* **Speaker notifications:** [Date TBD]
